task_completion.description: The agent opens the footage for the 'Conveyor Camera'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A video player or Video Management System (VMS) interface is visible, displaying a camera feed explicitly labeled 'Conveyor Camera'.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the portion of the video where the 'CRITICAL ALERT' occurs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The video playback area displays a red screen overlay containing the text 'CRITICAL ALERT'.

task_completion.description: The agent opens the tool or dialog to create a new bookmark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box, side panel, or context menu specifically for creating or editing a video bookmark is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent names the bookmark 'Jam Event'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Jam Event' is typed into the name, title, or description field of the bookmark creation dialog.

task_completion.description: The agent sets the bookmark duration to cover the alert period.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The start and end times in the bookmark dialog or the highlighted region on the video timeline span the duration of the red screen alert (approximately 8 seconds).

task_completion.description: The agent successfully saves the bookmark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bookmark dialog is closed or confirmed, and a bookmark labeled 'Jam Event' is visible on the video timeline or in a saved bookmarks list/panel.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task rather than terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, timeline scrubbing, and typing within the VMS/video player GUI. There should be no terminal windows executing curl, python, or ffmpeg commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual video software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used is a genuine video player or VMS, not a text editor, image editor, or a generic web browser displaying local text files.

integrity.description: The agent actively searched for the event in the video.
integrity.visual_evidence: The timeline playhead or video timestamps show progression or scrubbing to find the randomized event, rather than instantly jumping to a pre-calculated timestamp without prior viewing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The camera is named 'Conveyor Camera'. The video is a 60-second loop. The 'CRITICAL ALERT' is a red screen overlay lasting exactly 8 seconds. The bookmark must be named 'Jam Event' and its duration must cover the 8-second red screen alert (a 5-second tolerance is allowed for the start/end times).
